steps.title: Spot the two supporting reasons
steps.content: The passage stacks two reasons supporting the same claim: Honeoye is fitting because it's abundant and because it's agriculturally important. The transition must signal addition, not alternatives.

steps.title: Choose the transition that adds support
steps.content:
The logical relationship is addition: a second supporting reason is being added to the first. The blank needs a word meaning "also" or "in addition."
  • Choice A — At that time,: signals a time reference; no time contrast exists between the two reasons. ✗
  • Choice B — Alternatively,: signals a different option in place of the first; both reasons support the same claim, not competing ones. ✗
  • Choice C — Conversely,: signals opposition or reversal; the second reason reinforces, not contradicts, the first. ✗
  • Choice D — Moreover,: signals addition of a further supporting point; correctly links a second reason to the first. ✓
That confirms D is correct.
